scope: Wave-A acoustic unroll A/B on the coupled L2 d02 path (warmed per-step, min of cache-hit passes)
workload: L2 d02 66x159x44, guards ON, fp64, dt=10s, 10 acoustic substeps, 60-step non-radiation segment
methodology_note: warmed_per_step_ms = min over warm passes; the FIRST warm pass (new traced start_step) triggers a one-off XLA recompile (~2000-2800 ms/step) and is excluded; passes 2-3 are stable cache hits.
verdict: unroll=2 gives only ~0.9% warmed speedup (74.12 -> 73.44, within run-to-run noise) at +60% compile cost (116s -> 186s). On the COUPLED path the acoustic substep unroll buys essentially nothing because the step is NOT purely launch-bound on the acoustic fraction (Thompson sedimentation ~46% of the coupled step dominates; the v0.9.0 dycore-only 1.22x at unroll=4 does NOT translate to coupled). DEFAULT KEPT AT unroll=1; the hook remains for future dycore-only / Phase-2+ use.
